title: Performance Budget Ratchet Loop
summary: Improves performance using repeatable measurements and prevents backsliding with a ratcheted budget.
steps: Define the metric, measurement harness, warmup, sample count, and pass threshold before changing code.
Record a baseline under stable conditions and identify the largest measured contributor.
Change one lever at a time, rerun the same benchmark, and keep only changes that improve the target without regressions.
After each accepted improvement, ratchet the budget to the new proven level or threshold.
Add an automated performance guard where practical.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Performance Budget Ratchet Loop for [target_metric, budget, benchmark_command].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Define the metric, measurement harness, warmup, sample count, and pass threshold before changing code.
- Record a baseline under stable conditions and identify the largest measured contributor.
- Change one lever at a time, rerun the same benchmark, and keep only changes that improve the target without regressions.
- After each accepted improvement, ratchet the budget to the new proven level or threshold.
- Add an automated performance guard where practical.

Quality gates before completion:
- baseline recorded
- same benchmark conditions used
- statistical noise considered
- functional tests still pass
- budget guard added or justified

Stop when one of these is true:
- target metric met
- no safe improvement remains
- budget ends

Finish with:
- baseline and final measurements
- benchmark command
- accepted and rejected experiments
- guard configuration

Escalate for human approval before: quality tradeoffs, cache invalidation risk, infrastructure cost increase.
